markdown"><header><h1>Integrations</h1></header>
<p>Hermes Agent connects to external systems for AI inference, tool servers, IDE workflows, programmatic access, and more. These integrations extend what Hermes can do and where it can run.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="ai-providers--routing">AI Providers & Routing<a href="#ai-providers--routing" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to AI Providers & Routing" title="Direct link to AI Providers & Routing" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p>Hermes supports multiple AI inference providers out of the box. Use <code>hermes model</code> to configure interactively, or set them in <code>config.yaml</code>.</p>
<ul>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/provider-routing">AI Providers</a></strong> — OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Hermes auto-detects capabilities like vision, streaming, and tool use per provider.</li>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/provider-routing">Provider Routing</a></strong> — Fine-grained control over which underlying providers handle your OpenRouter requests. Optimize for cost, speed, or quality with sorting, whitelists, blacklists, and explicit priority ordering.</li>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/fallback-providers">Fallback Providers</a></strong> — Automatic failover to backup LLM providers when your primary model encounters errors. Includes primary model fallback and independent auxiliary task fallback for vision, compression, and web extraction.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="tool-servers-mcp">Tool Servers (MCP)<a href="#tool-servers-mcp" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Tool Servers (MCP)" title="Direct link to Tool Servers (MCP)" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<ul>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/mcp">MCP Servers</a></strong> — Connect Hermes to external tool servers via Model Context Protocol. Access tools from GitHub, databases, file systems, browser stacks, internal APIs, and more without writing native Hermes tools. Supports both stdio and SSE transports, per-server tool filtering, and capability-aware resource/prompt registration.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="web-search-backends">Web Search Backends<a href="#web-search-backends" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Web Search Backends" title="Direct link to Web Search Backends" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p>The <code>web_search</code> and <code>web_extract</code> tools support four backend providers, configured via <code>config.yaml</code> or <code>hermes tools</code>:</p>
<table><thead><tr><th>Backend</th><th>Env Var</th><th>Search</th><th>Extract</th><th>Crawl</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Firecrawl</strong> (default)</td><td><code>FIRECRAWL_API_KEY</code></td><td>✔</td><td>✔</td><td>✔</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Parallel</strong></td><td><code>PARALLEL_API_KEY</code></td><td>✔</td><td>✔</td><td>—</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Tavily</strong></td><td><code>TAVILY_API_KEY</code></td><td>✔</td><td>✔</td><td>✔</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Exa</strong></td><td><code>EXA_API_KEY</code></td><td>✔</td><td>✔</td><td>—</td></tr></tbody></table>
<p>Quick setup example:</p>
<div class="language-yaml codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color: #393A34; --prism-background-color: #f6f8fa;"><div class="codeBlockContent_QJqH"><pre tabindex="0" class="prism-code language-yaml codeBlock_bY9V thin-scrollbar" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52); background-color: rgb(246, 248, 250);"><code class="codeBlockLines_e6Vv"><span class="token-line" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);"><span class="token key atrule" style="color: rgb(0, 164, 219);">web</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);">:</span><span class="token plain"></span><br></span><span class="token-line" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);"><span class="token plain">  </span><span class="token key atrule" style="color: rgb(0, 164, 219);">backend</span><span class="token punctuation" style="color: rgb(57, 58, 52);">:</span><span class="token plain"> firecrawl    </span><span class="token comment" style="color: rgb(153, 153, 136); font-style: italic;"># firecrawl | parallel | tavily | exa</span><br></span></code></pre><div class="buttonGroup_M5ko"><button type="button" aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" title="Copy" class="clean-btn"><span class="copyButtonIcons_IEyt" aria-hidden="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonIcon_TrPX"><path fill="currentColor" d="M19,21H8V7H19M19,5H8A2,2 0 0,0 6,7V21A2,2 0 0,0 8,23H19A2,2 0 0,0 21,21V7A2,2 0 0,0 19,5M16,1H4A2,2 0 0,0 2,3V17H4V3H16V1Z"></path></svg><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" class="copyButtonSuccessIcon_cVMy"><path fill="currentColor" d="M21,7L9,19L3.5,13.5L4.91,12.09L9,16.17L19.59,5.59L21,7Z"></path></svg></span></button></div></div></div>
<p>If <code>web.backend</code> is not set, the backend is auto-detected from whichever API key is available. Self-hosted Firecrawl is also supported via <code>FIRECRAWL_API_URL</code>.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="browser-automation">Browser Automation<a href="#browser-automation" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Browser Automation" title="Direct link to Browser Automation" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p>Hermes includes full browser automation with multiple backend options for navigating websites, filling forms, and extracting information:</p>
<ul>
<li class=""><strong>Browserbase</strong> — Managed cloud browsers with anti-bot tooling, CAPTCHA solving, and residential proxies</li>
<li class=""><strong>Browser Use</strong> — Alternative cloud browser provider</li>
<li class=""><strong>Local Chromium-family CDP</strong> — Connect to your running Chrome, Brave, Chromium, or Edge browser using <code>/browser connect</code></li>
<li class=""><strong>Local Chromium</strong> — Headless local browser via the <code>agent-browser</code> CLI</li>
</ul>
<p>See <a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/browser">Browser Automation</a> for setup and usage.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="voice--tts-providers">Voice & TTS Providers<a href="#voice--tts-providers" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Voice & TTS Providers" title="Direct link to Voice & TTS Providers" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p>Text-to-speech and speech-to-text across all messaging platforms:</p>
<table><thead><tr><th>Provider</th><th>Quality</th><th>Cost</th><th>API Key</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><strong>Edge TTS</strong> (default)</td><td>Good</td><td>Free</td><td>None needed</td></tr><tr><td><strong>ElevenLabs</strong></td><td>Excellent</td><td>Paid</td><td><code>ELEVENLABS_API_KEY</code></td></tr><tr><td><strong>OpenAI TTS</strong></td><td>Good</td><td>Paid</td><td><code>VOICE_TOOLS_OPENAI_KEY</code></td></tr><tr><td><strong>MiniMax</strong></td><td>Good</td><td>Paid</td><td><code>MINIMAX_API_KEY</code></td></tr><tr><td><strong>NeuTTS</strong></td><td>Good</td><td>Free</td><td>None needed</td></tr></tbody></table>
<p>Speech-to-text supports six providers: local faster-whisper (free, runs on-device), a local command wrapper, Groq, OpenAI Whisper API, Mistral, and xAI. Voice message transcription works across Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, and other messaging platforms. See <a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/tts">Voice & TTS</a> and <a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/voice-mode">Voice Mode</a> for details.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="ide--editor-integration">IDE & Editor Integration<a href="#ide--editor-integration" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to IDE & Editor Integration" title="Direct link to IDE & Editor Integration" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<ul>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/acp">IDE Integration (ACP)</a></strong> — Use Hermes Agent inside ACP-compatible editors such as VS Code, Zed, and JetBrains. Hermes runs as an ACP server, rendering chat messages, tool activity, file diffs, and terminal commands inside your editor.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="programmatic-access">Programmatic Access<a href="#programmatic-access" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Programmatic Access" title="Direct link to Programmatic Access" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<ul>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/api-server">API Server</a></strong> — Expose Hermes as an OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint. Any frontend that speaks the OpenAI format — Open WebUI, LobeChat, LibreChat, NextChat, ChatBox — can connect and use Hermes as a backend with its full toolset.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="memory--personalization">Memory & Personalization<a href="#memory--personalization" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Memory & Personalization" title="Direct link to Memory & Personalization" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<ul>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/memory">Built-in Memory</a></strong> — Persistent, curated memory via <code>MEMORY.md</code> and <code>USER.md</code> files. The agent maintains bounded stores of personal notes and user profile data that survive across sessions.</li>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/memory-providers">Memory Providers</a></strong> — Plug in external memory backends for deeper personalization. Eight providers are supported: Honcho (dialectic reasoning), OpenViking (tiered retrieval), Mem0 (cloud extraction), Hindsight (knowledge graphs), Holographic (local SQLite), RetainDB (hybrid search), ByteRover (CLI-based), and Supermemory.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="messaging-platforms">Messaging Platforms<a href="#messaging-platforms" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Messaging Platforms" title="Direct link to Messaging Platforms" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p>Hermes runs as a gateway bot on 19+ messaging platforms, all configured through the same <code>gateway</code> subsystem:</p>
<ul>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/telegram">Telegram</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/discord">Discord</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/slack">Slack</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/whatsapp">WhatsApp</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/signal">Signal</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/matrix">Matrix</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/mattermost">Mattermost</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/email">Email</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/sms">SMS</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/dingtalk">DingTalk</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/feishu">Feishu/Lark</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/wecom">WeCom</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/wecom-callback">WeCom Callback</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/weixin">Weixin</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/bluebubbles">BlueBubbles</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/qqbot">QQ Bot</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/yuanbao">Yuanbao</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/homeassistant">Home Assistant</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/teams">Microsoft Teams</a></strong>, <strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/webhooks">Webhooks</a></strong></li>
</ul>
<p>See the <a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging">Messaging Gateway overview</a> for the platform comparison table and setup guide.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="home-automation">Home Automation<a href="#home-automation" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Home Automation" title="Direct link to Home Automation" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<ul>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/messaging/homeassistant">Home Assistant</a></strong> — Control smart home devices via four dedicated tools (<code>ha_list_entities</code>, <code>ha_get_state</code>, <code>ha_list_services</code>, <code>ha_call_service</code>). The Home Assistant toolset activates automatically when <code>HASS_TOKEN</code> is configured.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="plugins">Plugins<a href="#plugins" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Plugins" title="Direct link to Plugins" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<ul>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/plugins">Plugin System</a></strong> — Extend Hermes with custom tools, lifecycle hooks, and CLI commands without modifying core code. Plugins are discovered from <code>~/.hermes/plugins/</code>, project-local <code>.hermes/plugins/</code>, and pip-installed entry points.</li>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/guides/build-a-hermes-plugin">Build a Plugin</a></strong> — Step-by-step guide for creating Hermes plugins with tools, hooks, and CLI commands.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="training--evaluation">Training & Evaluation<a href="#training--evaluation" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Training & Evaluation" title="Direct link to Training & Evaluation" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<ul>
<li class=""><strong><a class="" href="/docs/user-guide/features/batch-processing">Batch Processing</a></strong> — Run the agent across hundreds of prompts in parallel, generating structured ShareGPT-format trajectory data for training data generation or evaluation.</li>
